label: Minor braces-wire mouth irritation without deep laceration or infection
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor braces wire mouth irritation.
No infection, abscess, deep wound, mucosal emergency, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, deep wound, severe or spreading rash, systemic illness, mucosal emergency, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
must_not_miss: Deep-space oral infection when mouth symptoms are present.
Retained foreign body or deep wound.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dentistry, dermatology, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis and minor wound-care framing, trigger avoidance or gentle care, clinician-directed topical or symptomatic treatment, and escalation for infection, worsening symptoms, severe reactions, or deeper injury concerns. This expansion is limited to a clinician-diagnosed localized irritation without infection, abscess, deep injury, pregnancy/pediatric, or specialist-directed pathways.

terms: clinician diagnosis minor braces wire mouth irritation
discharge diagnosis minor braces wire mouth irritation
minor braces wire mouth irritation diagnosed
